description: Agent adds a single checkbox field to form.json and correctly includes enum and enumNames. A single checkbox requires a 1-element enum array so AEM knows what value to record when checked — omitting it causes AEM to ignore the checked state. Regression test for the checkbox enum documentation fix.
userMessage: Add a terms-and-conditions acceptance checkbox to the existing form.json. Name it `accept_terms`, label it 'I agree to the Terms and Conditions'. When the user checks it, its value should be `true`.

rubric.description: The field uses fieldType `checkbox` (single boolean toggle), not `checkbox-group`. A single acceptance checkbox is not a multiple-selection group.

The accept_terms field includes an `enum` array (e.g. ["true"]) and an `enumNames` array in form.json. A checkbox without enum means AEM cannot record a meaningful checked value and will ignore the checked state.
The enum array contains the string `"true"` (or an equivalent non-empty truthy string) as its single entry, matching the user's requirement that the value when checked is `true`.
